title: Umami Chewy Cozy Saudi Stir-fry
description: A raw-friendly umami and chewy Saudi Arabian stir-fry featuring sake to add depth of flavor, ideal for a cozy dinner.

ingredients.name: Shrimp
ingredients.notes: Fresh or frozen, peeled and deveined

ingredients.name: Raw onion
ingredients.notes: Sliced finely for color and texture

ingredients.name: Shiitake mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Diced thinly, stems removed for chewiness

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: For umami and depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Sake
ingredients.notes: To deglaze the pan for added complexity

ingredients.name: Raw spinach
ingredients.notes: Washed and chopped finely for freshness

instructions: In a small bowl, combine kombu seaweed with soy sauce and set aside.
Heat sesame oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add pressed garlic cloves and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add shrimp to the pan and stir-fry for about 2 minutes or until pinked all over. Transfer to a plate, keeping the pan warm.
In the same pan, add sliced raw onion and shiitake mushrooms. Cook for about 5 minutes until vegetables soften slightly.
Pour in sake and kombu seaweed mixture. Stir quickly to combine ingredients and scrape any brown bits from the bottom of the pan.
Return shrimp to the pan with vegetable mixture. Stir to combine everything evenly.
Add raw spinach to the stir-fry, toss gently, ensuring it wilts without overcooking.
